steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum
steps.content: The interior angles of a triangle add to $180^\circ$. Use the two given angle measures and subtract them from 180.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content: The three interior angles of any triangle sum to $180^{\circ}$. We have $\angle P = 28^{\circ}$ and $\angle Q = 57^{\circ}$, so we need to find $\angle R$.

steps.title: Solve for angle R
steps.content:
Set up the equation: $$28^{\circ} + 57^{\circ} + \angle R = 180^{\circ}$$
Combine the known angles: $$85^{\circ} + \angle R = 180^{\circ}$$
Subtract $85^{\circ}$ from both sides: $$\angle R = 95^{\circ}$$
That matches the correct answer.
